question_text: A student rubs a polythene rod with a cloth. The rod becomes negatively charged. Explain why the rod attracts small pieces of paper, even though the paper is electrically neutral.
correct_answer: The negative charge on the rod repels electrons in the paper towards the far side, leaving the near side positively charged. The opposite charges attract, so the paper is pulled towards the rod.
explanation: When a charged object gets close to a neutral object, the charges inside the neutral object rearrange. Opposite charges are attracted to the rod while like charges are pushed away, creating an attractive force overall.
